message: @agent My geyser is leaking
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Here is what you need to do right now:

1. Turn off the geyser breaker on your DB board immediately.
2. Close the cold-water inlet valve going into the geyser (usually a blue-handled tap on the pipe feeding into it).
3. Place buckets or towels under the leak to limit water damage.

This ticket has been logged and a plumber will be assigned. Please let us know once you have completed these steps or if the situation gets worse.
author: AI Agent

message: I have a broken plug
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Sorry to hear that — I've logged this for you now. In the meantime, check your DB board to see if a breaker has tripped (a switch sitting in the middle position), and avoid using that socket until it's been looked at. The property team will be in touch to arrange a repair.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #67).
author: AI Agent

message: broken plug
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Sorry to hear that — I've logged this for you now. In the meantime, check your DB board for any tripped breakers and test the socket with a different appliance to confirm it's the socket and not the appliance itself. Avoid using that socket until it's been checked.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #68).
author: AI Agent

message: broken plug
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Sorry to hear that — I've logged this for you now. In the meantime, check your DB board to see if a breaker has tripped (it'll be in the middle position rather than fully up or down). If so, flip it off fully then back on. Avoid using that socket until it's been checked.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #69).
author: AI Agent

message: Can you please give me more details, where it, what is happening, please also send a photo as we need to issue an replacement unit
author: mc@tremly.com

message: broken door
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Sorry to hear that — I've logged this as a repair for the team to sort out. In the meantime, if the door won't close or lock properly, try lubricating the lock with WD-40 and check if the hinges have any loose screws. If the door can't be secured at all, please let me know and we'll treat it as urgent.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #70).
author: AI Agent

message: please send me an photo
author: mc@tremly.com

message: broken door
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Sorry to hear that — I've logged a repair request for the broken door at Unit 1. In the meantime, if the door won't close or lock properly, try not to leave the unit unattended until it's fixed. If you feel unsafe, please contact the property team directly.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #71).
author: AI Agent

message: @agent Hi
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Hi there! Thanks for reaching out. Can you tell me a bit more about the broken door — for example, is it not closing properly, is the lock damaged, or is there physical damage to the door itself?

Also, could you take a photo of the issue? It will help us assess it faster.
author: AI Agent

message: Hi
author: mc@tremly.com

message: @agent broken door
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Thanks for flagging this again. To help us get this sorted quickly, can you give us a bit more detail about what's happening with the door?

For example: is it not closing properly, is the lock damaged or jammed, or is there visible physical damage to the door?
author: AI Agent

message: Hi, I am the agent
author: mc@tremly.com

message: @agent Hi Agent, I am the tenant
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Hi! Thanks for reaching out. Can you tell me more about the broken door? For example, is it not closing properly, is the lock damaged or jammed, or is there visible physical damage to the door?
author: AI Agent

message: broken plug
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Which plug is it and what's happening — is it loose, sparking, or just not giving power?
author: AI Agent

message: the plug is not giving power
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Got it. Before I log this, try these quick checks: test with a different appliance to confirm the plug point is the problem and not the appliance itself. Also check your DB board for any tripped breakers — a tripped breaker will be in the middle or off position, just flip it firmly back to ON. If neither of those fixes it, let me know which room the plug is in and I'll get a ticket logged.
author: AI Agent

message: it is not giving power
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Which room is the plug in — bedroom, kitchen, bathroom, or somewhere else?
author: AI Agent

message: kitchen
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Got it, I've logged this for the maintenance team. In the meantime, check your DB board for a tripped breaker — look for any switch in the middle or off position and flip it firmly to ON. That sometimes restores a dead plug point.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #72).
author: AI Agent

message: Hi, I am the agent
author: mc@tremly.com

message: My gate motor is making funny sound
author: mc@tremly.com

message: What kind of sound is it making — is it grinding, clicking, beeping, or something else? And is the gate still opening and closing properly, or is it struggling to move?
author: AI Agent

message: Beeping
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Thanks for the extra detail. A beeping gate motor usually means the battery backup inside the motor is low or failing. This is a known issue and needs a technician to replace the battery in the motor unit. I've logged this for the property team to sort out. In the meantime, you can use the manual release on the motor (usually a key or lever) if the gate stops working completely.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #73).
author: AI Agent

message: Hi, agent here, when will you be avaliable for use to send someone out
author: mc@tremly.com

message: @agent Can tomorrow morning work
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
I don't have visibility into supplier schedules or availability, as no contractor has been assigned to this job yet. I'll flag this request to the property team to get someone booked in — they'll be able to confirm a time with you directly.

Is morning generally better for you, or are you flexible on timing?
author: AI Agent

message: My front door lock is broken
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Sorry to hear that — can you tell me a bit more about what's happening with the lock? Is the key not turning, the lock jammed, the handle broken, or can you just not get the door to lock at all?
author: AI Agent

message: The key is not turning
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Got it, thanks. A key that won't turn is often caused by a stiff or seized lock cylinder. While you wait for the repair, try spraying a little WD-40 or graphite spray into the keyhole and gently wiggling the key — this sometimes frees it up. I've logged the repair for the team to sort out.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #74).
author: AI Agent

message: Hi, Agent here. When can we send someone out
author: mc@tremly.com

message: I can meet someone tomorrow morning
author: mc@tremly.com

message: ok
author: mc@tremly.com

message: @agent where can I get a new key in Stellenbosch
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
For a new key in Stellenbosch, your best options are:

Locksmith shops — search "locksmith Stellenbosch" on Google Maps for the nearest one. They can cut keys on the spot and are usually quick.

Hardware stores like Builders Warehouse or a local hardware shop often have key cutting services too.

That said, since this is an active maintenance request, the property team should be arranging a locksmith to fix the seized cylinder. A new key may not solve the problem if the cylinder itself is faulty. I'd recommend waiting to hear back on the scheduled repair before getting a key cut, to make sure the new key will work with the fixed lock.
author: AI Agent

message: @agent  Can you look online for me
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
I'm not able to browse the internet, so I can't search online for you.

For finding a locksmith in Stellenbosch, your quickest options are to search Google Maps for "locksmith Stellenbosch" or call a local hardware store to ask if they offer key cutting.

That said, remember the property team should be arranging a locksmith to fix the seized cylinder — it may be worth waiting to hear back on that first before getting a new key cut.
author: AI Agent

message: What is my router internet password
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Your WiFi password is 0823068144. Let me know if you have any other questions!
author: AI Agent

message: What is the alarm password
author: mc@tremly.com

message: I don't have the alarm code on file for your unit. I've flagged this for the property team to get back to you. You can also reach them directly at tanja@klikk.co.za or WhatsApp 082 664 8071 / 082 306 8144.
author: AI Agent

message: My WiFi is down and I tried the password but still does not work
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Try rebooting the router first — switch it off, wait 30 seconds, then switch it back on. If it still does not connect after that, let me know and I will flag it for the property team to assist.
author: AI Agent

message: It still does not connect
author: mc@tremly.com

message: I'll flag this for the property team to assist you. In the meantime, double-check that the router lights are on and stable. If the router appears completely off or has a red light, it may be a broader connectivity issue on the building side that the team will need to sort out.
author: AI Agent

message: I think the router is faulty, can you log an maintenance issue
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Done, I've logged a maintenance ticket for the faulty router. The property team will be in touch to assist. In the meantime, you can reach them on WhatsApp at 082 664 8071 or 082 306 8144 if it is urgent.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #75).
author: AI Agent

message: My kitchen sink is leaking
author: mc@tremly.com

message: Which part of the sink is leaking — is it the tap dripping, water coming from under the sink (pipes or cabinet), or is it leaking around the drain?
author: AI Agent

message: Water is coming out from under the sync
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
Got it, water leaking under the sink has been logged for you. In the meantime, turn off the isolation valve under the sink (the small tap on the pipe going into the cabinet) to stop the flow. Put down some towels or a bucket to protect the cabinet floor. A plumber will be arranged to sort it out.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #76).
author: AI Agent

message: The geyser has burst
author: mc@tremly.com

message:
That's an emergency — act now to limit water damage. First, flip the Geyser breaker OFF on your DB board. Then close the cold-water inlet valve going into the geyser (it usually has a blue handle). Put towels or buckets under any dripping water while you wait for the plumber. I've logged this as urgent and the team will be in touch shortly.

We've logged this for the property team (maintenance request #77).
